dialogue_line_replacements.text: ¿Qué tú harías si pudieras vivir en cualquier país del mundo?
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: What would you do if you could live in any country in the world? (PR subject pronoun order)

dialogue_line_replacements.text: Claro que sí. Yo diría que el español es el mejor regalo que me dieron mis papás.
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: Of course. I would say Spanish is the best gift my parents gave me. (PR: papás more common than padres)

cultural_notes_overrides.formality: Puerto Rican Spanish uses the conditional identically to Mexican Spanish. '¿Podría ayudarme?' is equally polite in PR professional contexts.
cultural_notes_overrides.regional_variations: In PR casual speech, '¿Qué tú harías?' (subject pronoun before verb) is the natural word order for conditional questions. This is a defining feature of Caribbean Spanish grammar.
